{
  "action": "execute_tool",
  "tool": "python_repl",
  "tool_args": {
    "code": "import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.read_csv('test_data.csv')
print('Original shape:', df.shape)
numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=['number']).columns
outlier_mask = pd.Series(False, index=df.index)
for col in numeric_cols:
    Q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)
    Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
    outlier_mask = outlier_mask | ((df[col] < lower) | (df[col] > upper))
outlier_count = outlier_mask.sum()
print(f'Number of rows with at least one outlier: {outlier_count}')
df_clean = df[~outlier_mask]
print('Cleaned shape:', df_clean.shape)
df_clean.to_csv('test_data_cleaned.csv', index=False)
print('Saved cleaned data to test_data_cleaned.csv')
# Show summary of removed outliers per column
for col in numeric_cols:
    Q1 = df[col].quantile(0.25)
    Q3 = df[col].quantile(0.75)
    IQR = Q3 - Q1
    lower = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
    upper = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
    col_outliers = ((df[col] < lower) | (df[col] > upper)).sum()
    print(f'{col}: {col_outliers} outliers')"
  },
  "reasoning": "Load the CSV, detect outliers using IQR method for each numeric column, create a mask for rows containing any outlier, remove those rows, and save the cleaned dataset. This fulfills the sub-task of detecting and treating outliers.",
  "confidence": 0.9
}
